title: A knitted upper for a shoe with a moulded sole and a shoe
abstract: The present invention relates to an upper for a shoe, wherein said shoe comprises said upper and a sole connected to the lower edge of said upper via injection moulding, said upper comprising knitted areas with a low stitch density providing ventilation through the upper. The lower edge is knitted with a high stitch density, thereby limiting the flow of moulding material through the edge of the knitted upper. Thereby, a good and stable contact can be obtained when moulding a sole to the upper and it is not necessary to use reinforcement elements to secure the connection. In addition, when gluing premade soles to the upper, a better and more stable connection can be obtained due to the high density stitching and the larger contact surface.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . An upper for a shoe, wherein said shoe comprises said upper and a sole connected to the lower edge of said upper via injection moulding, said upper comprising knitted areas with a low stitch density providing ventilation through the upper, characterised in that said lower edge is knitted with a high stitch density, thereby limiting the flow of moulding material through the edge of the knitted upper. 2. An upper according to claim 1 , wherein the higher stitch density at the lower edge has a stitch density above 5-15 stitches pr. inch, preferably 10- 12 stitches pr. inch. 3. An upper according to claims 1 -2, wherein at least said knitted lower edge comprises a polyester. 4. An upper according to claim 3, wherein said polyester corresponds to the polyester in said moulding material. 5. An upper according to claims 1 -3, wherein at least said knitted lower edge comprises an acrylic material. 6. An upper according to claim 5, wherein said acrylic has a melting temperature above 160 degrees Celsius. 7. An upper according to claims 1 -6, wherein also the edge is adjacent to the lower edge and thereby adjacent to a sole line when the sole is knitted with a higher stitch density. 8. An upper according to claims 1 -7, wherein the entire upper is knitted. 9. An upper according to claim 8, wherein the knitted areas on said upper comprise knitted areas with different stitch densities to ensure specific stretch properties of the upper. 10. An upper according to claims 1 -9, wherein the upper comprises lacing holes for receiving a shoe lace and wherein said holes are surrounded by a knitting having a high stitch density. 1 1 . An upper according to claims 1 -10, wherein the upper comprises lacing holes for receiving a shoe lace and wherein the upper comprises lacing enforcement knitting areas having a high stitch density connecting the lacing holes and the lower edge to be connected to the shoe sole. 12. An upper according to claim 1 1 , wherein said lacing enforcement knitting areas are shaped as lines connecting the eyelet and the lower edge of the upper. 13. An upper according to claim 1 1 , wherein said lacing enforcement knitting areas are shaped as areas between the eyelet and the lower edge of the upper. 14. An upper according to claims 1 -13, wherein said upper comprises multiple layers of material and wherein one of said layers is knitted. 15. A shoe, wherein said shoe comprises an upper according to claims 1 -14.
